* What a week, huh?
* Monday was a bank holiday.  I wanted to find spicy soup and I’m pleased to say that I found some.
* Still recovering.  Each day I felt more myself, and each day returned some clarity which I hadn’t noticed I lost.  Unfortunately not in time for me to have potentially bungled something.
* At work I held a kick-off meeting which went OK, although my facilitation skills feel very rusty.  Gotta re-sharpen them.
* Built an “AI Agent”. Can honestly say I’ve worked on nothing quite like this before - and the results are good - although integrating it into workflows is proving challenging: Sometimes I miss Bertha.
* Had my second annual health check this week. It ran smoothly albeit long even if the phlebotomy left me feeling woozy.
* Friday the Yotsuya lxds drank orange wine and practiced zen buddhism.  I spelt my name right this time.
* Went to [“Ephemeral” at Such As](https://www.instagram.com/p/DPc7v_wiZje/) which was a hybrid data-visualisation & art installation.  It charted bloom dates in Japan, and shows how changing climate has been shifting them.  Very impressive work and I’m sad it was only open for a weekend because more people should see it.
* Hung out at the Indonesia festival in Yoyogi where I ate an amazing rendang which was the perfect level of spice, and witnessed a killer DJ set on CDJs from 2006.
* Went to see the [Sou Fujimoto](https://www.mori.art.museum/jp/exhibitions/soufujimoto/index.html) installation at the Mori Art Museum which was impressive. Hadn’t been to many architecture exhibitions before.  Preferred the actual buildings over the concepts which felt a bit impractical.  It included a scaled replica of the Grand Ring from the Osaka Expo, complete with a queue to enter it.
* Arisugawa-no-miya is full of beauty this time of the year.  I can’t wait for the 28th when we cross into Autumn proper.

![Collage of week 42, 2025](/pictures/img_4790-collage_original.jpeg "Collage of week 42, 2025")

• What a week, huh?
  • Monday was a bank holiday. I wanted to find spicy soup and I’m pleased to say that I found some.
  • Still recovering. Each day I felt more myself, and each day returned some clarity which I hadn’t noticed I lost. Unfortunately not in time for me to have potentially bungled something.
  • At work I held a kick-off meeting which went OK, although my facilitation skills feel very rusty. Gotta re-sharpen them.
  • Built an “AI Agent”. Can honestly say I’ve worked on nothing quite like this before - and the results are good - although integrating it into workflows is proving challenging: Sometimes I miss Bertha.
  • Had my second annual health check this week. It ran smoothly albeit long even if the phlebotomy left me feeling woozy.
  • Friday the Yotsuya lxds drank orange wine and practiced zen buddhism. I spelt my name right this time.
  • Went to “Ephemeral” at Such As which was a hybrid data-visualisation & art installation. It charted bloom dates in Japan, and shows how changing climate has been shifting them. Very impressive work and I’m sad it was only open for a weekend because more people should see it.
  • Hung out at the Indonesia festival in Yoyogi where I ate an amazing rendang which was the perfect level of spice, and witnessed a killer DJ set on CDJs from 2006.
  • Went to see the Sou Fujimoto installation at the Mori Art Museum which was impressive. Hadn’t been to many architecture exhibitions before. Preferred the actual buildings over the concepts which felt a bit impractical. It included a scaled replica of the Grand Ring from the Osaka Expo, complete with a queue to enter it.
  • Arisugawa-no-miya is full of beauty this time of the year. I can’t wait for the 28th when we cross into Autumn proper.

* Worked on an interesting problem over the week: How do you pragmatically iterate over a series without using loops/iterators/etc? The solution takes you back to fundamentals, and back to maths. Inductive proofs hold the answer. This was _very_ energising.
* Blood tests now all done: My arm is very bruised.
* On Friday evening I watched [Dune 2](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt15239678) with MT and D. The final exclamation of “Lisan al Gaib!” had the whole cinema laughing.
* Subsequently consumed the entire Dune cinematic universe ([Dune 1984](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0087182), [Frank Herbert’s Dune](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0142032), [Children of Dune](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0287839)), and have now made a pact to read the books. I am *hooked*.
* Saturday brought a long-awaited outlet for Karaoke, and I was made to sing Rasputin: Starting to think that the beard has got to go. Highlights were watching Mike Ehrmantraut and Feyd-Rautha Harkonnen on the dancefloor.
* Closed the week with a nightmarish journey to Birmingham involving three train tickets and standing around on Euston’s concourse waiting for an announcement that never came.

* Low energy start to the week: I had Monday off, and ended up taking part of Tuesday off too. I’ve been feeling a little under the weather, but I think now slowly on the mend.
* This week in data:

  * Individual shoes thrown away: 5
  * How long it took me to realise the clocks went back: 18 hours
  * Total of all payments made in cash: £17
* I have glasses now, sort-of.  I can see totally fine without them - but over the years I’ve developed an astigmatism and so they’re really helping with situations with contrasting bright lights.  Still figuring out when to wear them.
* In very exciting work news, CCC — which I’ve here and [there](https://alexwilson.tech/content/46dcc1c2-4791-4410-9065-e63318554381) since its kick-off last April — is now finally live, debuted this week by [Joanna Kao’s excellent article on AI-assisted accessibility tooling, and how they aren’t what they seem](https://www.ft.com/content/10489c19-aecc-43e4-947f-bfde497de7b9).  It was a monumental effort getting there and the change seems subtle, but is profound: Previously an article with inline functionality like that would not work on the main website or app.  The same is true of most news organisations and so I'm very excited to see where this capability goes!
* Working late on Wednesday (although, mostly stumped by R dependencies, so not that productive) turned into me spot-volunteering for Codebar where I packed a few bags and [got papped gossiping with JB on LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/posts/codebar-london_codebar-11thbirthday-thankyou-activity-7255154230716416000-znTv). Always a treat chatting to people who want to shift into tech.
* Building on success with applying an EQ, after exploring headphone options and seeing a few price-tags, I decided to fully restore my Sennheiser HD700s instead by replacing, cleaning and fixing nearly all the parts.  They look, feel and *sound* brand new and I’m chuffed at the upgrade for so little money spent.  As a bonus I also swapped the ear-tips on my AirPods Pros out ones made by [Comply](https://www.complyfoam.com/).
* Then I did the same with a few pairs of old sneakers (throwing a few others out). Part of a sole which hadn’t glued properly led me to trip up and it was *incredibly* painful.
* My Mum came to visit on Saturday to help out a bit with my flat and to set up her new phone. There were delays on both her trains - the route to Euston (as well as the station itself) [is in really dire shape](https://www.theguardian.com/business/article/2024/aug/29/peter-hendy-ex-network-rail-chair-threatened-firm-engineer-euston-criticism). Weather was OK and [Sendero](https://sendero-coffee.com/) was empty. Yasai for dinner.
* Sunday, L and I visited [“Looks Delicious!”](https://www.japanhouselondon.uk/whats-on/looks-delicious-exploring-japans-food-replica-culture/) at Japan House and assembled a fake bento box of fake foods. Fave detail: There are a handful of spilled foods hidden in the corners of the exhibit which *look* realistic. The spilled drink right by the entrance, however, was real.  Hit up Whole Foods for dinner and had the last dregs of their Goan vegetable curry which absolutely slapped.
* Since I had a few more hours at home than expected, I managed to watch the entirety of the [Alex Rider TV series](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt6964748/).  I read all the books as a kid and was surprised to see an adaptation was out there: It deviated heavily from the books and I see *why* the writers did that, but ultimately it went - I think - too close to every other bit of streaming-first young adult TV.  3/5.
* Ah yes, and in a bit of a feat, I 100%’d [Dave the Diver](https://www.igdb.com/games/dave-the-diver) including the recently released Godzilla and Balatro DLCs.  This game is *fantastic* and I cannot recommend it enough.  If you're still reading and haven't checked it out yet, forget Factorio and Balatro, and please check out Dave the Diver.  It's a love-letter to video-gaming and you have *not* experienced anything like it.

* Cleanly breaking weeks with travel seems to work great for me: It creates a neat time-bounded window, or a context if you will. But long-distance travel — which can span *days* — are a *lot* fuzzier.  These journeys can form their own chapters.
* This week in data:

  * Leon wraps consumed: 0
  * Hours slept: Not enough. :(
* In my case, a chapter involving some extremely pointy elbows  and my rib-cage on the the first long leg of my journey back: Being allocated a middle-seat is the opposite of winning the lottery.  And it involved some *extremely* tasty soup (at the pop-up Tin Kee) in Hong-Kong airport which almost made up for everything else...
* Long-haul flights get the travel out of the way, but, after you’ve done one or two (ever) they aren’t exactly exciting. You learn rules, tricks, optimisations to make the lost time  easier to manage.  You begin to trade these with your fellow travellers: This route has better food, this seat is easier to sleep in, this airline is much cheaper, this airport is to be avoided at all costs.  Long-haul travel becomes a part of the identity - and not one I enjoy embracing.
* Anyway, more importantly, my local Lawson is closed for refurbishment - I hope they finally fix their coffee machines.
* Back to work, and head-first into a little bit of framework investigation.  Going straight in after returning was a bit ambitious because the jet-lag is not being kind to me this time around and I can’t stop yawning.  I even did my ritual park visit and everything to no avail.
* First language class on Thursday. Went well. Jumped directly into hiragana which I had not expected to be *writing*. Need more practice!
* Mt Fuji made an appearance through the office window, which was a welcome surprise!
* Checked luggage and baggage carousels are pretty good and reliable these days, moreso with tech like air tags, so I don’t understand why folks do carry-on luggage and make embarkation/disembarkation a real chore for everyone else.
* Health markers going the right direction. 🤞
* Walking around at the weekend I randomly stumbled upon an outdoors concert and - gosh - firstly I’m impressed at how energetic the performers were considering their state of dress and how utterly bitterly cold it is right now - and secondly - maybe it’s a stylistic choice but it really didn’t sound in tune...
* And I spent a decent chunk of the rest of the weekend doing viewings. I’m pretty set on the area I want, but, I’m not set on any of the places I’ve seen yet.  Maybe next week will yield better results.
* Watched a few movies on the plane back but I don’t have enough space to review them properly, so here goes: [Girl, Interrupted (1999)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0172493): pretty good, [Coup! (2023)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt23793170): great, [Transformers One (2024)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt8864596): wonderful, [Ghostbusters: Frozen Empire (2024)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt21235248): ok.
* All that lugging around furniture last week + my bags on the way back seems to have messed up my arm slightly...
* Have a good week!

* It’s December, I now have a Christmas tree and it’s freezing.
* Consequences of a week about furniture?  This week I spent a lot of time at home waiting for deliveries, assembling furniture and preparing recycling materials from all the packaging.  More of it was that last one than I’m proud to admit.
* New monitor & desk means I can now work from home.  Actually, this time.
* This week I founded a new team which will be accountable for technical architecture.  On top of wrap-up activities for my previous area, I did requirement-gathering with my new stakeholders and I began developing a 3-pillar strategy.  Next step is writing a charter and drafting objectives - exciting stuff.
* Naturally I’m re-reading “[The Agile Comms Handbook](https://gilest.org/books/ach.html)” one of the most useful business books of all time.
* Went to the taco placed that must not be named.
* On Friday there was a major Cloudflare incident. You’ve probably heard about it.  This blog was unaffected, I’m not sure why.
* Wrote a 3000-word blog post this week, but missed my deadline after this incident took my tools offline.  Called out for my double-spacing during the review.
* After enjoying the experience that is McDonalds in Roppongi Hills on a Friday night, we went to the Japan opening of [Zootopia 2 (2025)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt26443597/).  If someone had told me that at its heart Zootopia is just an excuse to make animal puns for 2 hours, I would’ve watched the original one first.  Absolutely solid: 3.5/5.
* I’m now the proud owner of an olive tree.  It’s very small and I’m nervous about its upkeep.  I haven’t owned an actual plant in a long time.
* Inaugurating all my new furniture, I started playing [Mario Galaxy](https://www.igdb.com/games/super-mario-galaxy--3) for the first time ever and I’m actually really enjoying it?
* No blog this week? Hah.

* Fuelled by the introspection and retrospection of the last two weeks, I felt an enjoyable clarity all week. Unfortunately, at the same time, the weather did its darndest to punish me.
* This week in data:
  * Running: 158 minutes
  * Italian food eaten: 2 pizzas, 1 pasta
  * Bike parking: Free for First 2 Hourse
* Capability mapping is going well.  It’ll require a few passes to get right so I’m publishing early & often: Inaccuracies open a feedback loop for both improving the mapping, and, for finding areas for future improvements.  I'm starting to understand how governance can work.
* Papers Club reviewed [MCP](https://modelcontextprotocol.io/introduction) this week.  What I like is that there aren’t any new fundamental ideas: You aggregate APIs.  What *is* new: A new class of security vulnerability.  There’s lots of room for interesting development there.
* Had a first-pass at setting both my objectives and my expectations for the rest of the year.  It means doing a lot of (realistic) thinking about further developing as an IC, which is exciting.
* Reconnected with some old coworkers in [the best English-style pub I’ve been to yet](https://tabelog.com/tokyo/A1310/A131002/13214764/) - which was an absolute blast - with a *very* passable fish & chips.  We started late, so I wrapped early to catch a train and make a phone-call.
* Met up with some cool people in a Ginza sub-basement to play a few practice rounds of Tennis & Golf.  There were prizes but I didn’t win any.  My *golf swing* is better than I remember, but I much preferred the tennis. Which needs a lot of work.
* Always say yes to rainforest tea!  Especially when it tastes like strawberries!
* Yakiniku on Friday turned into regret on a Saturday.  Made some friends in a HUB along the way.
* The weekend marked many chores in the hood - instigated by a different important phone-call - and a fair amount of running: My recovery is getting better, but the weather really isn’t making it easy.
* Closed the week at a house-warming with the KP massive.  I love seeing how other people decorate.  Never thought I'd be talking about Product Validation at a house-warming.  Welcome to being 30, I guess.
* Confession is good for the soul.

* Huh, we're past the Summer Solstice.
* Tuesday morning I showed up bright and early at the radiology unit for a follow-up scan.  I really wasn't looking forward to the cannula but it was all fine, and the nurses added and removed it without leaving any bruising - which is a genuine first for any of the needles stuck in my arm this year.  The contrast dye made me feel nauseous — unfortunately I didn't pick up any super-powers from the process.  *Yet*.
* Work was a blur this week and I somehow picked up extra assignments while trying to mind my own business, but the main highlight was OKR day.  Just over three hours of presentation spread out over an entire day, leaving lots of room for coffees, an impromptu team lunch and a social in the tiny room above Watling Street.  Recharged (or drained?!) my social battery in a good way.
* Friday marked Ant day.  Happy Ant day.
* Picked up [Street Fighter 6](https://www.streetfighter.com/6) this weekend and on top of going through Arcade Mode, I've eclipsed the 100 online matches mark.  It's so goddamn fun, and is making for a nice break from Zelda.  It definitely feels like at least one character is unbalanced so I'm hoping Capcom will continue working at it.  What's most interesting is an entirely new build-your-own-fighter mode, which comes with a cringey single-player campaign.  Also the [music slaps](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_UKlVx16mx4), [like seriously](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nmv8HwUvc-k).
* *Sorry Kono, it's all about Han now.*
* My mum wrote a book reporting the history of Chamberlin Plc — called From Grenades to Griddles — and it came out this week. [You should check it out](https://www.amazon.co.uk/Grenades-Griddles-Everything-Between-Chamberlin/dp/191138385X).
* I've been working a bit on the underlying tech-stack for this blog, including finally caving and migrating to NPM Workspaces (not without their teething pains) and finally setting up Storybook.  I'm quite excited about that last bit because it's the step that I kept getting stuck on when attempting to remove Gatsby.

* We begin on a momentous day: the beginning of Pumpkin Spice season.  Naturally It’s a Bank Holiday too. I down a PSL from a business park and read some papers.
* Watched [Emily the Criminal (2024)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt15255876) on the train back to London, which featured an unusually emotive Aubrey Plaza for a 3/5.
* Nearly go to the [Bluesky meet](https://lu.ma/p73046vr), but LF & I get so lost in Liverpool Street that we arrive fashionably late.  Dan has started his talk and everyone seems engrossed, so instead of sheepishly barging in, we hit up a Beigel shop and head to the counting house.
* The week’s drama peaks as a nearby building experiences a full callout of the London Fire Brigade with 7 whole fire-engines arriving within minutes.  If there _was_ a fire, they put it out so quickly we never even saw it.  The whole thing was awe-inspiring to watch.
* Z and I end up trying out 3C’s new dinner menu. It’s a strange model: Instead of hiring a chef, the bar seems to have sublet their kitchen to a family-run restaurant.  Their Pad Thai is decent.  CL drops by for a catch-up (and an ill-advised Instagram Video Call) that runs very late.
* Spend Saturday working a video-player side-project in Canary Wharf.  It’s probably my last time there for a bit and I’m really going to miss those views!
* Closed the week with a DIY Persian dinner watching one of my most anticipated movies: [Borderlands (2024)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt4978420/).  It was disappointing: I’d hoped they’d do something like translate the [Handsome arc](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Borderlands:_The_Handsome_Collection) to a one-shot film (imagine Ryan Reynolds as Jack!)  Instead the film was messy, strangely cast and visibly rushed - but it had _beautiful_ visual effects: 2/5.
* Dinner slapped though: I make a mean olivier.

• We begin on a momentous day: the beginning of Pumpkin Spice season. Naturally It’s a Bank Holiday too. I down a PSL from a business park and read some papers.
  • Watched Emily the Criminal (2024) on the train back to London, which featured an unusually emotive Aubrey Plaza for a 3/5.
  • Nearly go to the Bluesky meet, but LF & I get so lost in Liverpool Street that we arrive fashionably late. Dan has started his talk and everyone seems engrossed, so instead of sheepishly barging in, we hit up a Beigel shop and head to the counting house.
  • The week’s drama peaks as a nearby building experiences a full callout of the London Fire Brigade with 7 whole fire-engines arriving within minutes. If there was a fire, they put it out so quickly we never even saw it. The whole thing was awe-inspiring to watch.

* Spent a lot of the week helping with various aspects of US election coverage next week. Some assorted tasks and a real deep-dive into web performance and maps, the latter which I’ve not done much work with before. Managed to get a 75% performance improvement in a crucial area which I’m quite happy with.
* This week in numbers:
  * Ebay listings created: 2
  * Red-meat consumed: 0 grams
  * Headphones thrown away: 1.5 pairs
* Starting to like React Hooks.  Yeah, I’ve not been thrilled at them until now.
* LF and I went to [Baron’s Bites](https://www.instagram.com/p/DBt44ZLoQdB), which was a League of Legends pop-up/takeover of [Butchies](https://butchies.co.uk/).  Was it worth the twenty-minute queue?  No.  But I got a badge, which I gave away later that same evening.  I paid £11 for their finest plant-based nuggets which started out strong and ended in regret.  The queue seemed friendly.
* The Apple Intelligence update dropped.  Um.  It’s not great, is it?  Here’s a doozy: “Cow sighting; desperate for cow manure”.  Here’s another: “One soup through a straw, deemed suboptimal, but went well without complications”.
* Halloween fell on Thursday and a few people at work dressed up.  I went dressed as myself — scary, I know.  I assumed that nobody would be dressed up on the second floor where I spent the day, but was pleasantly surprised at a convincing Markus Braun getup.  A close second place goes to the white rabbit on three.
* AG and I met up with YS who was in London on holiday, and after a little bit of mulling around and comparing notes on technology, we went to dinner.  I finally got to tick [Chatora City](https://maps.app.goo.gl/sfrRFz9tmDMEWhjU6) off the list.  Solid meal, all *slightly* smokey but in a clean, delightfully intentional way.  The spice was a little too much so we drowned our sorrows with mango lassi.
* The dual threat of halloween and MT’s birthday  added up to a Smurf-themed party at the weekend.  For the second time this week I went dressed as myself (why mess with a classic?).  Got there early, and pretty quickly a crowd reminiscent of the blue man group rocked up to The Kernel.  Other groups doing the Bermondsey Beer Mile loved it.  At some point I was anointed with Smurf-cap.
* The Thames Clipper is delightful: Really must take it more often.  Sat down, watching the sunset while drinking a breakfast tea.  Got through a chapter of the Staff Engineer's path.  Maybe good for doing some work while commuting?  
* [Challengers (2024)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt16426418) was a pretty decent tennis movie.  As many characters asked, “Are we talking about Tennis?” No, nothing in the movie the movie has anything to do with tennis, reader, and I don’t know why you’d think that.  A little too long, good soundtrack, 4/5.
* [Human Traffic (1999)](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0188674) is fun storytelling applied to a story where, really, nothing happens.  But not in a Seinfeld way — it’s more like the movie equivalent of hanging out in the smoking area all night at a rave.  Incredibly fun cast, *great* soundtrack, 4.5/5.

It’s been rather hot this week and for lack of air conditioning at home, I’ve been eating lots of spicy food – and espresso soft-serve — to survive.

* [Dan Dan Noodles](https://www.chinesefoodwiki.org/Dan_Dan_Noodles) (担担面) are the best things ever: A small amount of spicy soup and pork which comes out perfect nearly every time. Goes well paired with [Smashed Cucumber Salad](https://www.thekitchn.com/sichuan-smashed-cucumber-salad-recipe-23173272) (凉拌黄瓜) which — served cold and drizzled in Sichuan chilli oil — make the temperature melt away.
* [Kozhi Varutha](http://www.cooklikepriya.com/2013/09/varutha-kozhi-masala-spicy-chicken.html) is a new discovery — [Asha’s](https://www.ashasrestaurant.co.uk/) serves a similar fish curry which is my go to whenever I’m in Birmingham, and this is even easier to make. Lots of chilli and creamy chicken: Perfect.
* [Kara-Miso Ramen](https://foodandmeal.com/kara-miso-tonkotsu-ramen) (辛味噌ラーメン) for whatever reason isn’t that well known but is utterly delicious. I think I overdid the ginger and chilli because it came out a much deeper red, but, *oh my god* was it good. If you’re ever in Tokyo [be sure to check out the place I discovered it](https://www.tripadvisor.co.za/Restaurant_Review-g1066443-d1698900-Reviews-Karashibimisoramen_Kikanbo_Kanda-Chiyoda_Tokyo_Tokyo_Prefecture_Kanto.html).



With the heat however, haven’t got a whole lot done.

* For fun I’ve been writing small slack bots to automate journal entries and news digests, although maybe I should [re-write them in Rust](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Z3xPIYHKSoI)?
* [Resident Evil](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt9660182/) on Netflix was surprisingly good. Video-game stories don’t tend to work in film (*e.g. the [Resident Evil](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt0120804/) series of movies*) but this is a great exception. Highly recommended.
* Have you heard about the [curse of the ninth](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Curse_of_the_ninth)? Wondering if Gabe Newell is worried about a curse of the third in video-games.

Weeknotes: Of Bepsi and Frogs — Week 2, 2026

* Twelfth night burst into my apartment in the dead of the night and bundled my Christmas tree into a small box.
* Week 2 and I’m both still digesting last year and writing goals for this year. However after a self-inflicted nudge, I have now set my top-priority.
* For some system design, I learned a lot about how public holidays and time-off legally work in Japan. In short there are three-(ish) separate bodies of legislation which each do a different thing and name different days, but come together to give us weekends and bank holidays.  Although, interestingly, unless you’re a bank, none of these days is actually guaranteed.
* My birthday gift game is getting better.
* Over a delicious chicken lunch, I'm loaned some house-keys with a fob-attached AirTag. I know that the AirTag is there, obviously, but this is a rare opportunity to see how long it really takes for an phone to notice.  How long did it take my phone?  6+ hours.  Scary.
* Ridiculously efficient dental appointment on Friday.  In 45 minutes we did what normally takes 90+.  Getting there!
* Our boy Harry really knows how to make a sandwich.  I’ve said it before, and I’ll say it again.  This time?  Buffalo Chicken.
* Did you know that Ancient Egyptians did not drink cola or pepsi as these are modern beverages? Instead, according to ChatGPT, their primary drinks included **water**, **beer** and **wine**.  While not a direct replacement for soda, some modern Egyptian cafes offer drinks like tea, anise, sahlab, and hibiscus.
* Bright and early on Sunday, met up on a train platform bound for Tsukubasan where we hiked to the peak for hatsumode (and also the datacenter at the top).  Had a mild snafu getting down.
* Made some wagyu.  In the process my pan de-seasoned itself a bit.  How even does cooking work?
* Considering changing my gym.  My local is a bit tight, and I like the idea of classes.
* Infinite in mystery is the gift of the Goddess.

* This week someone said "it's almost March".  Mate.  I've barely noticed it's not January any more.  _Not cool_.
* The trouble with changing old systems is that sometimes you end up navigating a Kafkaesque confusion of package managers.  I wrote some code, improved some tests and while trying to deploy it all fell into a battle with nested dependencies.  At time of writing, the dependencies are winning.
* I'm glad that the promotions round is over.
* [Alex](https://alex.mullr.net/) bakes a pretty mean cookie!
* In a slightly welcome distraction, Mum stayed the weekend and we spent the time exploring Barnes and Fulham.  Ended up eating Golubtsy/Голубцы for dinner and being surprised at the season 2 finale of [Killing Eve](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt7016936/).
* [Nintendo direct this week](https://www.polygon.com/23589160/nintendo-direct-2023-february-date-time-zelda-advance-wars) was pretty cool.  Expect to hear more gushing about it imminently.
* Oh crap, I should probably get to doing that write-up of 2022 huh?
* Have had trouble sleeping this week.  I can't place if it's random, stress or something else.  But with this journey I've been on recently everything feels significant.
* In welcome news, I _should_ begin cognitive behavioural therapy next week.

* Reader, I ventured back into the world this week after recovering from - *something* - I caught returning from Bulgaria. Turns out that travel can be dangerous after-all.
* After not running much during the trip and then staying mostly indoors for a week, I ran [once](https://www.strava.com/activities/8042925740) this week and it was a real struggle. Lesson learned: stick to the habit - *even while travelling*. The upshot is that all my health stats are still tracking up.
* At work I spent a lot of time on recruitment-shaped activities for a few roles. On top of going through - *a lot* - of CVs, I designed a new interview process for a specialised role, leaning heavily on behavioural questions (with a few discussion-based technical questions) and [this GDS-inspired technique](https://www.annashipman.co.uk/jfdi/interviewing-fairly.html) and so far it’s performing quite well.
* In the evenings I’ve split my time between writing, coursework and drinking. Hoping to publish a few blog posts over the course of the rest of the year.
* This term I’m using three languages as part of my course - Python & JavaScript, which I’m comfortable with, and C++17 which is mostly new to me. Learning a new language, even formally ([with this textbook](https://www.goodreads.com/book/show/36708350-beginning-c-17)), is pretty refreshing so far.
* The [She-Hulk](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt10857160) finale was … *okay*. It happened just in time for [Love Is Blind Season 3](https://www.imdb.com/title/tt11704040) to start airing. At this point, it feels a bit rote and the editing is predictably excellent. Quite pleased that a few bets/calls I made early on have panned out so far. The tears thing was weird, wasn’t it?
* I stopped enjoying Twitter two years ago. Returning from a break the algorithm began working against me and my posts reached fewer people. Meanwhile my feed included negativity, drama and attention-grabbing threads - [because they drive engagement](https://www.cam.ac.uk/stories/viralpolitics). Musk’s [hostile takeover](https://www.theverge.com/2022/10/28/23428132/elon-musk-twitter-acquisition-problems-speech-moderation) this week doesn’t change much for me, I’ll still be disillusioned-yet-reachable there. However you can now also [find me on Mastodon](https://mastodon.social/@alexwilson), and of course, here ([via RSS](https://alexwilson.tech/feed.xml)).
